product_overview: A single-page interactive showcase for the Saha UI React component library that demonstrates theme switching, component examples, and toast notifications. The app is a demo playground (route: /) where users can browse components, toggle themes, and validate notification behavior.
core_goals: Provide an interactive gallery of Saha UI components so users can preview usage and visual states
Demonstrate runtime theme switching (light/dark/system) with persistence via ThemeProvider
Showcase application-level toast notifications (positioning, animation, auto-dismiss) via a ToastProvider
Keep the demo self-contained on a single route for easy local review and documentation reference
Surface common error states and debugging guidance for missing provider or storage issues

features.name: Theme Switching
features.description: Allow visitors to toggle the app theme between light, dark, and system modes using the header ThemeToggle; the ThemeProvider persists the preference when possible and updates component styles immediately.
user_flows: Navigate to / -> Click the theme toggle control in the header -> Observe UI colors and component styles update to active theme -> Refresh the page -> Observe theme persists to the selected choice

Open browser with localStorage disabled -> Navigate to / -> Click the theme toggle control in the header -> Observe UI colors and component styles update to active theme -> Refresh the page -> Observe theme falls back to system/default because persistence is unavailable

features.name: Component Gallery Exploration
features.description: Browse the component showcase on the root route to inspect usage, interact with example controls, and verify rendered visual states and behaviors for Saha UI components.
user_flows: Navigate to / -> Scroll through component sections -> Interact with example controls (buttons, inputs, dialogs, etc.) -> Verify rendered behavior and visual states match the examples

Navigate to / -> Scroll through component sections -> Interact with example controls (buttons, inputs, dialogs, etc.) -> See a component unresponsive or render an error -> Open browser console -> Observe error stack trace to aid debugging

features.name: Toast Notification Experience
features.description: Trigger and validate application-level toast notifications (position, animation, auto-dismiss) using the demo examples; confirm ToastProvider renders and that toasts behave as documented.
user_flows: Navigate to / -> Trigger components that emit toast notifications -> Validate toast position, animation, and auto-dismiss behavior -> Observe toast disappears after the configured timeout

Navigate to / -> Trigger components that emit toast notifications -> See no toast appear -> Inspect app structure to verify ToastProvider is mounted -> Observe missing provider or related warning in the console

routes.description: Main component showcase page rendering Saha UI examples

features.name: Theme Switching
features.description: Users can switch between light and dark themes using the header toggle
user_interactions: Click the theme toggle control in the header
Observe UI colors and component styles update to active theme

features.name: Component Gallery Exploration
features.description: Users browse and interact with comprehensive Saha UI component examples
user_interactions: Interact with example controls (buttons, inputs, dialogs, etc.)
Verify rendered behavior and visual states

features.name: Toast Notification Experience
features.description: Application-level toast provider supports notification rendering and display behavior
user_interactions: Trigger components that emit toast notifications
Validate toast position, animation, and auto-dismiss behavior

known_limitations.issue: No multi-page routing; app is a single showcase route
known_limitations.impact: Route-level navigation and auth-flow scenarios are out of scope

known_limitations.issue: No backend API integration in default demo flow
known_limitations.impact: API contract and backend integration tests are not applicable for core demo path
